fields.personalityIdealsBonds:
Zodiac: Sea Seraph, Thalia
Age: 27

Loves cheese!

Personality Trait: I talk to animals as if they’re people.
                               I collect strange treasures.

Ideal: Freedom. No tide, king, or captain should decide where someone belongs.

Bond: I protect animals and wild places because they cannot         always protect themselves.

Flaw: My curiosity gets me into trouble.
          I get restless if I stay in one place too long.
          Scared of small and closed of spaces
fields.appearance:
Averag height. 
Long wavy brown/red hair that is often braided with feathers and small trinkets.
Sea green eyes, and sun kissed skin. 
She wears practical pirates clothing. Favoring practical leather and weathered fabrics over anything fancy. A fitted leather corset and belts keep her gear close, while her sleeves from her white blouse are rolled up for freedom of movement. Leather pants with some small rope wrapped around the legs and the Boots are scuffed and strapped, ready for ship decks or rocky shores alike.
Small treasures collected from distant shores like shells, bits of sea glass, and unusual coins hang from cords and pouches on her belt.
A persistent seagull is often found nearby.
At first glance Mara appears to be a carefree pirate chasing adventure, but beneath that free-spirited exterior lies a woman deeply connected to the ocean and fiercely protective of the creatures and wild places she calls home.
She carries herself with confidence and there is something unmistakably wild about her
fields.equipmentInventory:
* Herbalism Kit
* Traveler’s Clothes
* Bedroll
* Waterskin
* Rations (7)
* Rope
* Net
* Cheese
* Sling
* Sling Stones (30)
* Arrows
* Quiver
* Druidic Focus (sprig of mistletoe)

feats.name: Natural Blubber
feats.details: As the Selkie became one with their seal friends, they adapted to the chilling cold of the water by adopting a thin layer of natural blubber below their skin, granting them resistance to cold damage.

feats.name: Friend of Fauna
feats.short: Able to speak to all animals.
feats.details: The Selkie are fey that are naturally friendly towards animals. Although they’ve bonded specifically with seals, they are innately able to speak with all animals.

feats.name: Fey Ancestry
feats.short: Advantage on saving throws against Charmed.
feats.details: Selkies are a type of fey. Therefore, they have Advantage on saving throws that they make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

feats.name: Natural Adept
feats.short: Advantage on foraging and can sleep with no shelter.
feats.details: Selkies have abandoned the Frontier of Dawn, willingly choosing to survive in the harsh circumstances of the Material Plane in order to live with their chosen companions: the seals. Therefore, Selkies have become adept at survival. They have Advantage on foraging and are used to sleeping outside without shelter.

feats.name: Selkie's Nature
feats.short: Wis or Seal; swim of 40, AC13+Wis, Temp hp 3xlvl
feats.details:
Though it began with donning the pelts of seals that they had befriended, forced evolution caused the Selkies to bond with the pelts and companions on a genetic level. In the present day, Selkies can choose to follow their instincts or fight them with a Wisdom saving throw (DC 10), as they are instinctually driven to transform into a seal when they enter a body of water.

Transforming into a seal, they gain the ability to breathe underwater for up to an hour and gain a swimming speed of 40 ft.

While in the shape of a seal, the Selkie’s Armor Class is equal to 13 + their Wisdom modifier if that total is higher than the Natural Seal’s AC of 15. In addition, Selkies gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to three times their level.

feats.name: Favored Enemy
feats.short: Cast Hunter's Mark without spell slot.
feats.details:
You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared.
You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a Long Rest.

The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Ranger Levels, as shown in the Favored Enemy column of the Ranger Features table.

feats.name: Weapon Mastery
feats.details:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For Example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Scimitars and Longswords.

spells.name: Hunter's Mark
spells.effect: 1d6 force damage with every hit. Adv. on any Wis check to find target.
spells.details:
Level 1 Divination (Ranger)

Casting Time: Bonus Action
Range: 90 feet
Components: V
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 hour

You magically mark one creature you can see within range as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Force damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. You also have Advantage on any Wisdom (Perception or Survival) check you make to find it.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before this spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action to move the mark to a new creature you can see within range.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 3–4 (up to 8 hours) or 5+ (up to 24 hours).

spells.name: Alarm
spells.effect: You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won’t set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is audible or mental:
spells.details:
Casting Time: 1 minute or Ritual
Range: 30 feet
Components: V, S, M (a bell and silver wire)
Duration: 8 hours

You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won’t set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is audible or mental:

Audible Alarm. The alarm produces the sound of a handbell for 10 seconds within 60 feet of the warded area.

Mental Alarm. You are alerted by a mental ping if you are within 1 mile of the warded area. This ping awakens you if you’re asleep.

spells.name: Ensnaring strike
spells.effect: 1d6 piercing damage at the start of each turn.
